name: AWeber API
what_it_does: Provides OAuth 2.0-secured REST API access to AWeber's email marketing platform for managing subscriber lists, contacts, campaigns, and autoresponder sequences targeted at small businesses.
use_cases: Add new subscribers to an AWeber list when a user signs up via a custom form or landing page outside AWeber's native forms
Update subscriber custom field values (e.g., purchase date, plan type) to enable segmented follow-up autoresponder sequences
Retrieve subscriber activity data (opens, clicks, unsubscribes) for a list to feed into a business reporting dashboard
Unsubscribe or delete a contact across all lists on receipt of a GDPR/CCPA deletion request
Create broadcast messages programmatically when a new content piece is published and needs announcement to subscribers
not_for: High-volume or enterprise email marketing — AWeber is designed for small businesses and lacks the advanced segmentation, multivariate testing, and throughput of enterprise platforms
Complex multi-step marketing automation with conditional branching — AWeber's automation is simpler than ActiveCampaign, HubSpot, or Klaviyo
Transactional email sending at scale — AWeber is a marketing email platform, not a transactional email infrastructure
best_when: A small business agent needs to sync subscriber data between a website, CRM, or e-commerce platform and AWeber's email lists with straightforward list management operations.
avoid_when: The use case requires advanced behavioral segmentation, high-throughput sending, complex automation logic, or real-time event-triggered emails at enterprise scale.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow required for all API access. No API key option. Tokens must be refreshed using the refresh token before expiry (access tokens expire in 5 minutes, refresh tokens in 90 days). For server-to-server automation, the initial OAuth consent must be performed manually once by the account owner.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: up to 500 subscribers, 3,000 emails/month; API access available on all plans.
pricing.paid_tiers: Lite from $12.50/mo; Plus from $20/mo; Unlimited from $899/mo. Pricing scales with subscriber count.
pricing.notes: API is available on all tiers including free. Rate limits are the main constraint for agent use cases on lower-tier plans.

performance.rate_limits: Approximately 120 requests per minute; lower limits apply to lower-tier plans. Bulk subscriber operations count as multiple requests.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses are returned but documentation of error codes is dated and incomplete. OAuth errors follow standard RFC 6749 format which is well-understood. Some API endpoints return inconsistent error shapes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET and DELETE are idempotent. POST subscriber creation is not — adding an existing subscriber email returns an error rather than silently succeeding. Agents must handle duplicate subscriber errors gracefully.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth access tokens expire after only 5 minutes — agents must implement aggressive token refresh logic or face frequent authentication failures mid-run
The initial OAuth authorization requires interactive user consent in a browser; server-to-server automation requires manual one-time setup by the account owner
AWeber's API documentation is dated (some examples reference OAuth 1.0a which is deprecated); verify against the v1.0 reference, not older blog posts
Subscriber additions are subject to AWeber's anti-spam review; bulk imports may be flagged for manual review, blocking automated list-building workflows
Rate limit errors (429) do not include a Retry-After header — agents must implement exponential backoff without guidance from the response

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 is good but the very short access token lifetime (5 minutes) creates operational complexity without corresponding security benefit in server-to-server contexts. Refresh token lifetime of 90 days is reasonable.
